heading: Attorney General relieved of responsibility
label: 288k Attorney General relieved of responsibility
notes: Amendments2003—Subsec. (b). made technical amendment to reference in original act which appears in text as reference to .Pub. L. 108–7section 530D of title 282002—Subsec. (b). added subsec. (b) and struck out former subsec. (b) which read as follows: “The Attorney General shall notify the Counsel with respect to any proceeding in which the United States is a party of any determination by the Attorney General or Solicitor General not to appeal any court decision affecting the constitutionality of an Act or joint resolution of Congress within such time as will enable the Senate to direct the Counsel to intervene as a party in such proceeding pursuant to .”Pub. L. 107–273section 288e of this title

children.text: be relieved of any responsibility with respect to such representational service;
have no authority to perform such service in such action or proceeding except at the request or with the approval of the Senate; and

transfer all materials relevant to the representation authorized under to the Counsel, except that nothing in this subsection shall limit any right of the Attorney General under existing law to intervene or appear as amicus curiae in such action or proceeding.section 288c(a) of this title

children.label: b
children.text: The Attorney General shall notify Counsel as required by .section 530D of title 28
